perturbation.original_text: (g) Not to send Auditee ’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidential Information at any time outside India for the purpose of storage, processing , analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee.
perturbation.changed_text: (g) Not to send Auditee ’s audit information or data and/or any such Confidential Information at any time outside India.
perturbation.explanation: Removing "for the purpose of storage, processing, analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee" creates an overbroad restriction. Previously, sending data outside India was permissible with consent for processing, etc. Now, any transfer outside India is prohibited, even if the Auditee explicitly approves it for a legitimate purpose. This directly contradicts any implied right of the Auditee to control its data and consent to its transfer, creating in-text contradiction with section 5(d).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Exceptions. The Confidentiality obligations as enumerated in Article 2 of this Agreement shall not apply in following cases : (a) Which is independently developed by Auditor or lawfully received from another source free of restriction and without breach of this Agreement; or (b) After it has become generally available to the public without breach of this Agreement by Auditor ; or (c) Which at the time of disclosure to Auditor was known to such party free of restriction and evidenced by document s in the possession of such party; or (d) Which Auditee agrees in writing is free of such restrictions. (e) Which i s received from a third party not subject to the obligation of confidentiality with respect to such Information;
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clause "for the purpose of storage, processing , analysis or handling without the express written consent of the Auditee" from section (g). This creates a contradiction with section 5(d), which states "Which Auditee agrees in writing is free of such restrictions". Previously, sending data outside India was permissible with consent for processing, etc. Now, any transfer outside India is prohibited, creating an in-text contradiction with the Auditee's right to provide written consent.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Remedies. Auditor acknowledges that an y actual or threatened disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by Auditor would be a breach of this agreement and may cause immediate and irreparable harm to Auditee or to its clients; Auditor affirms that damages from such disclosure or use by it may be impossible to measure accurately; and injury sustained by Auditee / its clients may be impossible to calculate and compensate fully. Therefore, Auditor acknowledges that in the event of such a breach, Auditee shall be entitled to specific perfo rmance by Auditor of its obligations contained in this Agreement. In addition Auditor shall compensate the Auditee for the loss or damages caused to the auditee actual and liquidated damages which may be demanded by Auditee . Liquidated damages not to excee d the Contract value. Moreover, Auditee shall be entitled to recover all costs of litigation including reasonable attorneys’ fees which it or they may incur in connection with defending its interests and enforcement of contractual rights arising due to a b reach of this agreement by Auditor . All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and in addition to any other rights or remedies under any applicable law, at equity, or under this Agreement, subject only to any limitations stated herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Remedies. Auditor acknowledges that an y actual or threatened disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by Auditor would be a breach of this agreement and may cause immediate and irreparable harm to Auditee or to its clients; Auditor affirms that damages from such disclosure or use by it may be impossible to measure accurately; and injury sustained by Auditee / its clients may be impossible to calculate and compensate fully. Therefore, Auditor acknowledges that in the event of such a breach, Auditee shall be entitled to specific perfo rmance by Auditor of its obligations contained in this Agreement. Moreover, Auditee shall be entitled to recover all costs of litigation including reasonable attorneys’ fees which it or they may incur in connection with defending its interests and enforcement of contractual rights arising due to a b reach of this agreement by Auditor . All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and in addition to any other rights or remedies under any applicable law, at equity, or under this Agreement, subject only to any limitations stated herein.
perturbation.explanation: Removing "In addition Auditor shall compensate the Auditee for the loss or damages caused to the auditee actual and liquidated damages which may be demanded by Auditee . Liquidated damages not to excee d the Contract value." removes the specific mention of liquidated damages and their limit. While the text still allows recovery of 'all costs of litigation', it removes the pre-agreed-upon compensation mechanism for a breach. This creates uncertainty since it contradicts the idea that damages are difficult to measure accurately, leaving the auditee with no predefined compensation in Section 6.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Remedies. Auditor acknowledges that an y actual or threatened disclosure or use of the Confidential Information by Auditor would be a breach of this agreement and may cause immediate and irreparable harm to Auditee or to its clients; Auditor affirms that damages from such disclosure or use by it may be impossible to measure accurately; and injury sustained by Auditee / its clients may be impossible to calculate and compensate fully. Therefore, Auditor acknowledges that in the event of such a breach, Auditee shall be entitled to specific perfo rmance by Auditor of its obligations contained in this Agreement. Moreover, Auditee shall be entitled to recover all costs of litigation including reasonable attorneys’ fees which it or they may incur in connection with defending its interests and enforcement of contractual rights arising due to a b reach of this agreement by Auditor . All rights and remedies hereunder are cumulative and in addition to any other rights or remedies under any applicable law, at equity, or under this Agreement, subject only to any limitations stated herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a specific remedy of 'actual and liquidated damages' with a limit, offering clarity on compensation. The changed text removes this pre-agreed compensation mechanism, creating a contradiction with the initial acknowledgment that damages are difficult to measure accurately. By removing the liquidated damages provision, the Auditee no longer has a predefined compensation amount, creating an inconsistency and potentially leading to disputes over the extent of damages recoverable. This directly conflicts with the idea of easily calculable compensation and creates uncertainty and potential unenforceability, making it a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Need to Kno w. Auditor shall restrict disclosure of such Confidential Information to its employees and/or consultants with a need to know (and advise such employees and/or consultants of the obligations assumed herein), shall use the Confidential Information only for the purposes set forth in the Agreement, and shall not disclose such Confidential Information to any affiliates, subsidiaries, associates and/or third party without prior written approval of the Auditee . No information relating to auditee shall be hosted o r taken outside the country in any circumstances.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Need to Kno w. Auditor shall restrict disclosure of such Confidential Information to its employees and/or consultants with a need to know, shall use the Confidential Information only for the purposes set forth in the Agreement, and shall not disclose such Confidential Information to any affiliates, subsidiaries, associates and/or third party without prior written approval of the Auditee . No information relating to auditee shall be hosted o r taken outside the country in any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: Removing '(and advise such employees and/or consultants of the obligations assumed herein)' weakens the 'Need to Know' clause. The Auditor is still required to limit access but is no longer explicitly obligated to inform those employees/consultants of their confidentiality obligations. This contradicts the overarching goal of protecting Confidential Information because uninformed employees could inadvertently breach the agreement. This also contradicts section 2(k), which states that all employees have signed a Non-Disclosure agreement, which implies that the auditor should advise them.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (k) Make sure that all the employees and/or consul tants engaged to undertake any audit on its behalf have signed the mandatory non -disclosure agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly required the Auditor to advise employees/consultants of their confidentiality obligations. The changed text removes this explicit obligation. The contradicted text, section (k), states that all employees and/or consultants have signed a non-disclosure agreement. The removal of the explicit advising obligation in the changed text creates a contradiction. While the employees/consultants may have signed an NDA, the auditor is no longer explicitly required to remind them of their obligations, potentially undermining the purpose of the NDA. This creates a direct conflict and a strong contradiction, as the change directly breaks compatibility with section (k)'s implicit assumption that the auditor would inform them.

perturbation.original_text: 19. This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accordance with the laws of India. In the event of dispute arises between the parties in connection with the validity, interpretation, implementation or alleged breach of any provision of this Agreement , the parties shall attempt to resolve the dispute in good faith by senior level negotiations. In case, any such difference or dispute is not amicably resolved within forty five (45) days of such referral for negotiations, it shall be resolved through arbitration process, wherein both the parties will appoint one arbitrator each and th e third one will be appointed by the two arbitrators in accordance with the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The venue of arbitration in India shall be (please choose the venue of dispute resolution as the city) or where the services are provided. The proceedings of arbitration shall be conducted in English language and the arbitration award shall be substantiated in writing and binding on the parties. The arbitration proceedings shall be completed within a period of one hundred and eighty (180) da ys from the date of reference of the dispute to arbitration.
perturbation.changed_text: 19. This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accordance with the laws of India. In the event of dispute arises between the parties in connection with the validity, interpretation, implementation or alleged breach of any provision of this Agreement , the parties shall attempt to resolve the dispute in good faith by senior level negotiations. In case, any such difference or dispute is not amicably resolved within forty five (45) days of such referral for negotiations, it shall be resolved through arbitration process. The proceedings of arbitration shall be conducted in English language and the arbitration award shall be substantiated in writing and binding on the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing ", wherein both the parties will appoint one arbitrator each and th e third one will be appointed by the two arbitrators in accordance with the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The venue of arbitration in India shall be (please choose the venue of dispute resolution as the city) or where the services are provided. The arbitration proceedings shall be completed within a period of one hundred and eighty (180) da ys from the date of reference of the dispute to arbitration." while also stating the resolution will be an 'arbitration process', The procedure for initiating and conducting arbitration is now undefined. There are no instructions regarding the number of arbitrators or the venue. The omission of the procedural details for arbitration makes the dispute resolution clause unenforceable and contradictory to any claims that arbitration will occur after Section 19.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19. This Agreement is governed by and shall be construed in accordance with the laws of India. In the event of dispute arises between the parties in connection with the validity, interpretation, implementation or alleged breach of any provision of this Agreement , the parties shall attempt to resolve the dispute in good faith by senior level negotiations. In case, any such difference or dispute is not amicably resolved within forty five (45) days of such referral for negotiations, it shall be resolved through arbitration process. The proceedings of arbitration shall be conducted in English language and the arbitration award shall be substantiated in writing and binding on the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a clear arbitration process, including the number of arbitrators, the method of their appointment, the venue, and the timeframe for completion. The changed text removes these details while still mandating an 'arbitration process'. The contradicted text which is the same as the changed text, now lacks the procedural elements initially agreed upon, thus creating a contradiction by implying an arbitration process without the necessary framework, rendering it unenforceable and conflicting with the expectation of a defined dispute resolution mechanism as described in the original text and implied in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: 17. Survival. Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall survive till perpetuity even after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. Survival. Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall survive even after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "till perpetuity", the duration of the confidentiality obligations becomes unclear. This contradicts the assumption that confidentiality is meant to be perpetual as indicated by the original text. It is now unclear how long the auditor must maintain the confidentiality, creating an in-text contradiction within Section 17. Further it contradicts the purpose of the NDA (Non-Disclosure Agreement) - is it for life or is there a time limit.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Survival. Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall survive even after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that confidentiality obligations survive 'till perpetuity'. The changed text removes 'till perpetuity', making the duration of confidentiality obligations unclear. This directly contradicts the original intent of perpetual confidentiality, as now the agreement lacks a specific timeframe. The Contradicted Text mirrors the Change Text, indicating the same point in the document is now contradictory, due to the Omission of the original 'perpetuity' term.
